title: Opinion - The Kathmandu Post
authors: By Pramod Regmi & Edwin van Teijlingen
summary: Editorial United Nepali Congress is in everyone’s interest By The country has never needed a party with strong democratic credentials in the opposition more than it does today. Nepal’s food system is under strain. Are we prepared to safeguard our future? By Madhukar Upadhya Nepal urgently needs a comprehensive, long-term strategy to safeguard its food security. AI: Modern-day Prometheus or monster
content:
Editorial United Nepali Congress is in everyone’s interest By The country has never needed a party with strong democratic credentials in the opposition more than it does today. Nepal’s food system is under strain. Are we prepared to safeguard our future? By Madhukar Upadhya Nepal urgently needs a comprehensive, long-term strategy to safeguard its food security. AI: Modern-day Prometheus or monster? By Kim Seong-kon AI will constantly evolve due to its deep-learning capability. Someday, it may overcome its limits and outsmart humans. Such a phenomenon will inevitably change the whole terrain of our society. Doing away with exams, ushering in assessments By Salome Bhatta If exams fail to serve their very purpose, how should we actually assess learning? Open-air burning is choking our cities. It’s time to tackle it By Sophia L Pandé People must be educated about the harm caused by open-air burning to themselves and others. The essence of statecraft is not economics By Sucheta Pyakuryal Economic prosperity is part and parcel of statecraft, not the be-all and end-all of it. The American-Israeli war on Iran is the quest for dominance By Kashif Islam The talks in Pakistan aim not for peace but to buy time and weaken Iran permanently. Editorial Correction centres are crucibles of chaos. Still, they are redeemable. By When the state deprives children of basic needs, it is manufacturing criminals Wealth, professionalism and political responsibility in Bikram Sambat 2082 By Deepak Thapa The nationwide celebration of a young Madheshi woman as Deputy Speaker of HoR offers hope. Improving ambassador appointments By Jhabindra Aryal It is vital to appoint career diplomats as ambassadors to advance our national interests. Editorial On Shah government’s accommodative commitment framework By The government should try to listen to the broadest possible section of the population. Beyond history: Unlocking new avenues in Nepal-UK cooperation By Chandra Ghimire Now is the moment to develop strategic actions aligned with technical competence and national interests. Nepal’s migrant workers: Data the new government can’t ignore By Pramod Regmi & Edwin van Teijlingen Undocumented workers, arguably among the most vulnerable, remain invisible in official statistics. Editorial Failing provinces could spell disaster for traditional parties By Further weakening of provinces will bring more bad news for old parties. Breaking down Nepal’s health budget: Where are the funds going? By Khim Lal Devkota Without reforms, Nepal’s federal health system risks remaining centralised. Enduring legacy of Ambedkar. Lessons for Nepal By Prashant Negi & Rup Sunar RSP signals reform, but Ambedkar’s framework questions Dalit inclusion within Nepal’s evolving governance. Editorial Addressing Nepal’s vulnerability to measles By The country cannot afford a wait-and-see approach to deadly diseases such as measles. How the government silently abandoned the proposed apology to Dalits By Mitra Pariyar The dismissal of the state’s apology to Dalits discredits both the government and the RSP. Tourism, culture and development: Three pillars of Nepal’s soft power By Prasanta Kumar BK Now is the time to unlock Nepal’s soft power globally and ensure its progress isn’t delayed. Editorial Can PM Shah redefine Nepal-India ties? By The trip could be a rare moment when history stops dictating the future of Nepal-India ties.
